description: Send a direct message (DM) to a Twitter/X user by handle. Navigates to the Messages section, searches for the recipient, and types the message with human-like delays. Only sends to existing DM conversations or users who allow DMs.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: Twitter/X DMs are at x.com/messages. A new DM conversation is started via the compose DM button (a[data-testid='NewDM_Button'] or similar). The recipient search input is within the modal. The message input is div[data-testid='dmComposerTextInput'] — a contenteditable div. Sending is done via button[data-testid='dmComposerSendButton'] or Enter key. Users who have DMs restricted may not be searchable.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use data-testid for compose button, recipient search, message input, and send button — all stable across redesigns. Fall back to aria-label selectors. The DM compose modal has distinct selectors from the main messages thread view.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Message input requires char-by-char human typing at 60-120ms. Must click compose DM button explicitly. Recipient search requires human-like typing with 80-150ms delays. Wait for search results to appear before clicking recipient.
reasoning.llm_learnings: Twitter may show 'This person limits who can message them' if DMs are restricted. Send button may be disabled until a recipient is selected. After sending, verify message appears in conversation thread.
reasoning.performance: Navigation 3s + modal open 2s + recipient search 5s + typing variable + send 3s. Short message (~100 chars): ~20-30s total.

recipient_handle.description: Twitter/X handle of the recipient (with or without @)

message_text.description: Text of the direct message to send
message_text.example: Hey! I wanted to follow up on the OAuth3 discussion.

steps.description: Verify authenticated — if redirected to login flow, abort with SESSION_EXPIRED
Click the New Message / Compose DM button to open recipient search modal
Wait for recipient search input to appear in compose modal
Click recipient search input to focus it
Type recipient handle in search field with human-like delays
Wait for user search results to appear in dropdown
Click the first matching user result to select recipient
Click Next to open the DM compose view with selected recipient
Wait for DM message compose input to be ready
Click message compose input to focus it
Type DM message text with human-like delays
Click Send button to deliver the DM
Verify DM was sent without errors

message_text.example: Hey! I wanted to follow up...

error_handling.session_expired: Detect x.com/i/flow/login redirect — abort with SESSION_EXPIRED, re-run session login recipe first
error_handling.recipient_not_found: If TypeaheadUser results are empty after typing handle, abort with RECIPIENT_NOT_FOUND
error_handling.dm_restricted: If 'limits who can message them' banner appears, abort with DM_RESTRICTED
error_handling.compose_not_loaded: Retry wait_for_selector with 15s timeout; if still fails, navigate to x.com/messages/compose directly
error_handling.element_not_found: Log selector tried, capture screenshot, try fallback_selector if defined
error_handling.rate_limited: If DM rate limit detected, wait 60s then retry once
